meta.name: star-lite-starter
meta.description: Starter content for a Star-Lite Docs site.

content.tagline: A drop-in Starlight-style docs theme for EmDash CMS.

children.text: A drop-in documentation theme that brings the power of
to allow for a fully featured frontend editing expereince for documentation instead of using static MDX files on disk.
Starlight-style layout and theme with collapsible sidebar, and table of contents
Ctrl+K search bar and modal using
Expressive Code syntax highlighting with bundled Night Owl themes
Drop-in emdash plugin — one integration, no schema or plugin setup
Zero-config self-seeding — first request creates the
menu, and a welcome page with Houston
Menu-driven sidebar — edit navigation from the emdash admin, no need to rebuild for any changes you make while retaining full control over the menu
14 rich Portable Text block types from Starlight: hero, tabs, cards, asides, steps, badges, file trees, icons, link buttons, code, images, and raw HTML
— toggle Edit in the emdash toolbar, click any text to edit in place, then hit Save in the toolbar. Thats it, your changes are live and ready to view.
— hover any block in edit mode to reveal an Edit button and open an inline property editor for all the block fields, no need to take a trip to the admin page.

— click "Edit as MD" in the header to switch to a full-page markdown textarea with a save button. All content and blocks work with markdown so you can edit existing content and blocks as well as add new ones using the same markdown workflow you are used to in Starlight. Click save and see the markdown rendered immediantly on the page.
— one-click clipboard copy of any page as markdown using the header button

content.code:
git clone https://github.com/gruntlord5/star-lite-docs.git
cd star-lite-docs/starter
bun install
bun run dev

children.text: to create your admin user, then visit
to see the themed welcome page.
Add to an existing emdash site
This installs the package and wires
. Block components, the Houston welcome page image, and route conflict warnings are all handled automatically at build time.
The starter includes a Dockerfile for production deployments. Data and uploads persist across container restarts via Docker volumes.

content.code:
# Quick start — build and run in one command
bun run docker

children.text: This builds the image and runs it at
For production, run the container detached with a restart policy:

content.code:
docker build -t my-docs .

docker run -d \
  --name my-docs \
  --restart unless-stopped \
  -p 4321:4321 \
  -v my-docs-data:/app/data \
  -v my-docs-uploads:/app/uploads \
  my-docs

children.text: Star-Lite adds three editing modes on top of emdash, all implemented as a plugin with no emdash patches.
in the emdash toolbar (bottom of the page)
Click any text — headings, paragraphs, hero taglines, card titles, badge text, etc.
button appears in the toolbar — click to persist and reload
Inline editing works on any element with a
attribute. Star-Lite tags all its block components with this attribute, and standard Portable Text blocks are preprocessed into
With Edit mode on, hover any block to see a dashed blue outline and an
button in the top-right corner. Clicking it opens an inline form showing all the block's properties (tagline, image src, action URLs, etc.). Save persists the changes and reloads.
in the header (visible when Edit mode is on). This switches to a full-page markdown textarea. Custom blocks appear as
fences and round-trip safely. Click Save to persist, or Cancel to return to the rendered view.
| Splash hero with title, tagline, image, and action buttons |
| Standalone image with editable src and alt |
| Raw HTML passthrough (also used for preprocessed text blocks) |
| Content card with optional icon and color |
| Navigation card with title, description, and link |
| Callout box — note, tip, caution, or danger |
| Inline icon from the Starlight icon set |
| Styled link rendered as a pill button |
Astro components are available at subpath imports:
Content is stored as Portable Text in the
table — the database is the source of truth.
catch-all route looks up a published page by slug, preprocesses blocks (images, headings with TOC anchors, tables, and standard text blocks are all converted to
On first boot, the middleware runs
menu, and a welcome page. This is idempotent — existing data is never overwritten.
The toolbar Save button is injected client-side via a
handler that watches the emdash toolbar's status area with a
element provides the toolbar with entry context (collection, id, status) without triggering click-to-admin redirects.
